titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.7

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=361 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:19:00.641251+00 | translation moa=362 status=live modified=2021-04-05 08:19:02.521808+00

div.p:
Alex'. dei gratia Rex Scott'. Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue
salutem . Sciatis quod concessimus Monachis de Melros . vt terram suam
de Moll' in perpetuum habeant in liberum forestum . Quare fir
miter prohibemus . ne quis in dicta terra sua secet aut uenetur
sine eorum licencia . super nostram plenariam forisfacturam decem
libratum . Test'. Waltero . Olifard' . Iusticiario . Laodonie . Rogerum
Auenel . Dauid Marscallo . Apud . Edinburc' . Vicesimo Octa
uo die Maii . Anno . Regni . Domini regis Vicesimo secundo

Alexander, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting. Know that I have conceded to the monks of Melrose that they may have their own land of Mow for ever in free forest. Wherefore we strictly forbid that anyone should fell timber or hunt without their permission, on pain of our full forfeiture of ten pounds. As witnesses: Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian; Roger Avenel; David the Marischal. At Edinburgh, on the twenty-eighth day of May, in the twenty-second year of the reign of the lord king.
